ip_outcome_note: Card name, game source (FFXIII-2), and visual description (large dark-armored beast with purple energy accents) directly match the Luminous Puma enemy from Final Fantasy XIII-2 (Augusta Tower 200 AF). Internal Square Enix IP — no external IP risk. Verified via Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom) and gamercorner.net FFXIII-2 bestiary.

trivia_bullets.text: The Luminous Puma originates from Final Fantasy XIII-2 (2012), where it appears as an enemy encountered in Augusta Tower (200 AF). It belongs to the Monster class — a tameable creature category in XIII-2's Paradigm Pack system, where defeated monsters can be recruited into the player's party. [Final Fantasy Wiki: Luminous Puma, via WebSearch snippet; gamercorner.net FFXIII-2 monsters, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom): Luminous Puma — WebSearch snippet
gamercorner.net FFXIII-2 bestiary: Luminous Puma — WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: As a FFTCG Backup (card 8-080C, Common), Luminous Puma's card effect lets the controller sacrifice it to either power up all Forwards by 2000, or grant one Forward 5000 Power — in exchange for Earth CP and Light CP. This makes it a budget utility piece in Earth/Light mixed builds rather than a standalone threat. [WebSearch snippet: FFTCG Luminous Puma card effect summary]
sources: WebSearch snippet: FFTCG Luminous Puma card effect — Crystarium-adjacent summary

trivia_bullets.text: FFXIII-2's Augusta Tower is set in a far-future timeline (200 years After Fading) where rogue artificial intelligence PSICOM and proto-fal'Cie have taken control. The Luminous Puma and similar mechanically-armored beasts in the Augusta Tower zone reflect the setting's cyberpunk-industrial aesthetic — far removed from the pastoral or mythological creature designs of earlier XIII locations. [Final Fantasy Wiki: Augusta Tower / FFXIII-2, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom): Augusta Tower (FFXIII-2) — WebSearch snippet

related_cards.note: Near-identical enemy type in FFXIII-2 — same puma family, different zone and variant
